response:
The project is divided into 5 phases with man-hours forming a geometric progression. Let the first phase require x man-hours. The sequence will be x, 2x, 4x, 8x, 16x. The total man-hours is x + 2x + 4x + 8x + 16x = 31x. Given that 31x = 310, x = 10. The man-hours for each phase are 10, 20, 40, 80, 160.  

The initial timeline for the project is 20 weeks. Each phase will now take 1.5 times longer. The new duration for each phase is 1.5 times the original. The total new duration is 1.5 multiplied by the original total duration. The new total project duration is 30 weeks.  

The project manager has to adjust the schedule based on the new time estimates. The resource allocation plan must be reviewed to ensure that the extended timeline is accommodated. The coordination between teams may need to be re-evaluated. The project manager must communicate the changes to all stakeholders. The team must be informed about the revised timeline. The project manager must ensure that all teams understand the new schedule. The project manager must monitor the progress closely. The project manager must track the performance of each team. The project manager must ensure that the project stays on track. The project manager must maintain the quality of the deliverables. The project manager must ensure that the budget remains within limits. The project manager must manage the risks associated with the extended timeline. The project manager must ensure that the project meets its objectives. The project manager must maintain the morale of the team. The project manager must ensure that the project is completed on time. The project manager must ensure that the project is completed within the budget. The project manager must ensure that the project meets the quality standards. The project manager must ensure that the project is completed successfully. The fortune of the project depends on the effective management of the extended timeline.
